identity.name: Onboarding Step
identity.definition: An Onboarding Step is a single, actionable task within an Onboarding Checklist that guides a user to perform a specific activation behavior.

representations.heading: Card layout
representations.description: How an Onboarding Step appears as a standalone card

examples.title: Schedule Your First Star Assessment
examples.description: Set up a Star Reading or Star Math assessment for your class to establish baseline scores.

examples.title: Create Your First Class
examples.description: Set up your class roster so students can be assigned work and assessments.

examples.title: Review the Reports Dashboard
examples.description: Explore the reports dashboard to understand how student data is organized.

attributes.description: Position within the parent checklist sequence.

attributes.name: Status

examples.title: Schedule Your First Star Assessment
examples.description: Set up a Star Reading or Star Math assessment for your class to establish baseline scores. Baseline data drives personalized learning paths and helps identify students who need intervention.
examples.successMetric: At least one Star assessment scheduled for a class

attributes.name: Step Title
attributes.description: Short, action-oriented title.

attributes.name: Step Description
attributes.description: Full explanation of the step and its purpose.

attributes.name: Order
attributes.description: Position within the parent checklist.

attributes.name: Status
attributes.description: Current state: not-started, in-progress, complete, or skipped.

attributes.name: Target Object
attributes.description: The object type this step directs the user to.

attributes.name: Target CTA
attributes.description: The specific CTA on the target object that completes this step.

attributes.name: Completion Method
attributes.description: How completion is determined: manual (user clicks done) or auto-detect (system detects the target CTA was performed).

attributes.name: Help Link
attributes.description: URL to an Educator Academy Module or help article related to this step.

attributes.name: Icon
attributes.description: Material Icon name for visual identification.

attributes.name: Success Metric
attributes.description: Description of what constitutes successful completion of this step.

attributes.name: Completed Date
attributes.description: When the step was marked complete.

attributes.name: Skipped Date
attributes.description: When the step was skipped by the user.

stories.title: Complete Onboarding Step
stories.benefit: I make progress toward setting up my account and the checklist reflects my advancement.
stories.whenClause: I perform the target action for a step (e.g., schedule my first assessment),
stories.thenClause: the step is automatically marked complete if auto-detect is enabled, or I can manually mark it done, and the parent checklist progress updates.

stories.title: Skip Optional Step
stories.benefit: I can focus on the setup tasks most relevant to my immediate needs without being blocked.
stories.whenClause: I skip a step I don't need right now,
stories.thenClause: the step is marked skipped, the checklist progress still advances, and I can undo the skip later if I change my mind.

businessRules.title: Ordering
businessRules.description: Steps within a checklist have a fixed order determined by the system. Users cannot reorder steps.

businessRules.title: Auto-Detection
businessRules.description: Steps with completionMethod 'auto-detect' monitor the target object for the target CTA being performed. Once detected, status changes to complete automatically.

businessRules.title: Manual Completion
businessRules.description: Steps with completionMethod 'manual' require the user to explicitly click a 'Mark as done' button.

businessRules.title: Skip Behavior
businessRules.description: Skipped steps count toward checklist progress but are visually distinguished. Users can undo a skip at any time to return the step to not-started.

businessRules.title: Status Immutability
businessRules.description: Once a step is marked complete, it cannot be reverted. Skipped steps can be un-skipped (returned to not-started) but completed steps are permanent.

businessRules.title: Checklist Propagation
businessRules.description: Every step status change (complete, skip, undo-skip) triggers a recalculation of the parent Onboarding Checklist's progress percentage.

businessRules.title: Help Link Association
businessRules.description: Each step may link to an Educator Academy Module for deeper training on the step's topic. This is optional and managed by the content team.

states.name: Not Started
states.description: Step has not been attempted by the user

states.name: In Progress
states.description: User has begun but not completed the target action
states.triggers: User clicks Start Step or begins the target CTA

states.name: Complete
states.description: User has performed the target action successfully

states.name: Skipped
states.description: User has chosen to skip this step

relationships.mechanics: Steps belong to a single Onboarding Checklist. Status changes on a step propagate to recalculate checklist progress.
relationships.cardinality: Many-to-one. Each Step belongs to exactly one Checklist; a Checklist contains multiple Steps.
relationships.sorts: Steps within a checklist: by order (ascending, fixed).
relationships.dependencies: If a Checklist is dismissed, its steps are hidden but retain their status for analytics.

relationships.mechanics: A step may link to an Educator Academy Module as supplementary training. Completing the linked module may auto-complete the step.
relationships.cardinality: Many-to-one. A Step may link to one Module; a Module can be linked from multiple Steps across checklists.
relationships.filters: By whether the linked module is complete.
relationships.dependencies: If the linked module is unpublished, the help link falls back to a generic help URL.

relationships.mechanics: Steps are generated per-user as part of their onboarding checklist. Each teacher has their own set of step instances.
relationships.cardinality: Many-to-one. Each Step instance belongs to one Teacher's checklist; a Teacher has one checklist with multiple Steps.
relationships.filters: By teacher role (teacher vs. admin) to determine step set.
relationships.dependencies: If a teacher is deactivated, their step data is retained for analytics but no longer visible in active views.

allAttributes.name: Step Title
allAttributes.description: Short, action-oriented title describing what the user should do.

allAttributes.name: Step Description
allAttributes.description: Brief explanation of why this step matters and what it accomplishes.

allAttributes.name: Order
allAttributes.description: 1-based position within the parent checklist sequence.

allAttributes.name: Status
allAttributes.description: Current state: not-started, in-progress, complete, or skipped.

allAttributes.name: Target Object
allAttributes.description: The object type this step directs the user to interact with.

allAttributes.name: Target CTA
allAttributes.description: The specific CTA on the target object that completes this step.

allAttributes.name: Completion Method
allAttributes.description: How completion is determined: manual or auto-detect.

allAttributes.name: Help Link
allAttributes.description: URL to related Educator Academy Module or help article.

allAttributes.name: Icon
allAttributes.description: Material Icon name for visual identification of the step.

allAttributes.name: Success Metric
allAttributes.description: Description of what constitutes successful completion.

allAttributes.name: Completed Date
allAttributes.description: Timestamp when the step was marked complete. Null if not complete.

allAttributes.name: Skipped Date
allAttributes.description: Timestamp when the step was skipped. Null if not skipped.

structure.evidence: An Onboarding Step has structured attributes: title, description, order, status, target object, target CTA, completion method, help link, icon, and success metric. You can design a detail view for a Step.

instances.evidence: 'Schedule Your First Star Assessment' (order 1, auto-detect, targets Assessment), 'Create Your First Class' (order 2, auto-detect, targets Class), 'Review the Reports Dashboard' (order 3, manual, targets Report). Each user's checklist has 5-10 Step instances.

purpose.evidence: Onboarding Steps guide new users through critical setup tasks, reducing time-to-value and ensuring key activation behaviors occur. Teachers interact with steps by starting, completing, or skipping them, and the parent checklist tracks overall progress.

sipValidation.verdict: Domain activity object. Atomic unit of onboarding guidance that drives user activation within a structured checklist.

synonyms.notes: Generic term; Step emphasizes the sequential, guided nature
Emphasizes the setup/configuration nature of onboarding steps
Conflates with general task management; Step is onboarding-specific
Descriptive term used in help articles and tooltips

shapeshifterMatrix.description: An individual onboarding step shown as a row within the checklist. Users start, complete, or skip steps as they work through setup.
The complete step record — description, target object, completion method, and help link. Users get guidance on what to do and how to complete each onboarding task.
